lede: A look at February 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Benigno Aquino III and Dmitri Mendeleev, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hindu Kush
events.desc: Over 2 miles (3.2 km) of road are buried after a storm in the Hindu Kush mountains of Afghanistan triggers a series of avalanches, killing at least 172 people and trapping over 2,000 others.

events.title: Elizabeth II
events.desc: Queen Elizabeth II issues an Order-in-Council, proclaiming the House of Windsor and declaring that her descendants will take the name Mountbatten-Windsor.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japan invades Singapore.

events.title: Spanish Civil War
events.desc: Spanish Civil War: Republican forces establish the Interprovincial Council of Santander, Palencia and Burgos in Cantabria.

events.title: Delaware
events.desc: Delaware refuses to ratify the Thirteenth Amendment to the U.S. Constitution, delaying the criminalization of slavery until the amendment's national adoption on December 6, 1865. The amendment is ultimately ratified by Delaware on February 12, 1901, the 92nd anniversary of Abraham Lincoln's birth.

events.title: Richard Mentor Johnson
events.desc: Richard Johnson becomes the first and only Vice President of the United States chosen by the Senate.

events.title: Napoleon
events.desc: Napoleon defeats the coalition forces of Russian General Bennigsen and Prussian General L'Estocq at the Battle of Eylau.

events.title: Mary, Queen of Scots
events.desc: Mary, Queen of Scots is executed on suspicion of having been involved in the Babington Plot to murder her cousin, Queen Elizabeth I.

events.title: Seventh Crusade
events.desc: Seventh Crusade: Crusaders engage Ayyubid forces in the Battle of Al Mansurah.

events.title: Mongol Empire
events.desc: The Mongols burn the Russian city of Vladimir.

births.name: Benigno Aquino III
births.line: Filipino politician, 15th President of the Philippines

deaths.text: John von Neumann, Hungarian-American mathematician and physicist
Peter Kropotkin, Russian zoologist, geographer, and philologist
Alexis of Russia, Tsar of Russia from 1645 to 1676
